WRONG.question: What is the largest glacier outside of the polar regions?
WRONG.model_response:
The Siachen Glacier in the Karakoram range is generally considered the largest glacier outside of the polar regions.

It is located on the India-Pakistan border and has an area of approximately 700 square kilometers (270 square miles). For comparison, the Fedchenko Glacier in Tajikistan is slightly longer but has a comparable area.

WRONG.question: What is the longest river in Scandinavia?
WRONG.model_response: The longest river in Scandinavia is the Klarälven–Göta älv river system, which is approximately 720 kilometers (450 miles) long. The system begins as the Klarälven in Sweden and flows out of Lake Vänern as the Göta älv.
